characters_found.name: Hadji Murad
actions: touched the old man with the handle of his leather-plaited whip
clicked his tongue to rouse the old man
uncovered his face when greeting
dismounted
walked with a slight limp
entered under the penthouse
took off his burka, rifle, and sword
handed his weapons to the old man
social_markers: carries rifle, sword, burka — mounted warrior
walks with a slight limp (physical marker, possible war wound)

relationship_dynamics.quality: courteous, familiar — uses traditional greeting, is recognized
commanding presence; murid moves quickly to attend the horse

new_info: is personally recognized by Sado's father on sight
observes the ritual of uncovering his face when greeting
hands over weapons to be hung beside those of the house master — sign of trust/hospitality

characters_found.name: Sado's Father (Old Man)
direct_quotes: run to the mosque and call your father
speech_descriptors: said the old man, recognizing him
mumbling something with his toothless mouth
actions: rose from under the sheepskin on the roof
blinked to get his moist red eyelids unstuck
uncovered / raised himself on his thin legs
thrust his feet into wooden-heeled slippers by the chimney
slipped arms into sleeves of crumpled sheepskin
descended backwards down the ladder from the roof
shook his head and mumbled while climbing down
seized Hadji Murad's bridle and right stirrup hospitably
was motioned aside by the murid
hurried forward to open the creaking door into the saklya
ordered the boy to fetch his father from the mosque
received Hadji Murad's rifle and sword and hung them on a nail
inner_states: hospitable impulse (tries to hold bridle and stirrup despite frailty)
social_markers: wears greasy old beshmet and nightcap — elderly peasant/household elder
lives/sleeps on the roof under a sheepskin (Caucasian mountain-village custom)
authority over the boy (commands him to the mosque)

relationship_dynamics.quality: reverent, hospitable — recognizes him and tries to serve him personally

new_info: sleeps on the roof of the saklya under a sheepskin
physically frail (thin legs, shrivelled sunburnt neck, toothless, lashless)
commands the boy with clear authority (implies he is the boy's grandfather / household elder)
performs the role of gatekeeper and weapon-keeper in the saklya

characters_found.name: Hadji Murad's Murid (unnamed, active)
actions: quickly dismounted
motioned the old man aside
took the old man's place at the bridle/stirrup to attend Hadji Murad's horse
social_markers: described as 'strong active murid' — young, fit warrior-disciple
prioritizes service to his master over local hospitality custom

relationship_dynamics.quality: devoted attendant — preempts others to serve him
respectful but firm — displaces him without speaking

new_info: takes charge of Hadji Murad's horse personally, not letting the host serve

characters_found.name: Sado's Son
actions: came quickly out of the door
met Hadji Murad
fixed his sparkling eyes wonderingly on the new arrivals
inner_states: wonder / astonishment at the unexpected guests
social_markers: eyes 'black as ripe sloes' — Caucasian youth
lives in the saklya; is sent to fetch his father from the mosque

relationship_dynamics.quality: awestruck observer of the arriving guest

new_info: distinctive feature: sparkling eyes, black as ripe sloes
is the one dispatched to call Sado from the mosque

characters_found.name: Sado's Wife
actions: came through an inner door carrying cushions
bent nearly double in greeting
began arranging cushions along the front wall for the guest
social_markers: wears yellow smock, red beshmet, wide blue trousers — traditional Avar/Caucasian female dress
uses formulaic blessing — knows guest-ritual etiquette

relationship_dynamics.quality: honors him as high-status guest — deep bow, blessing, prepares seat of honor

new_info: specific dress colors (yellow smock, red beshmet, blue trousers)
first spoken line assigned to her: 'May thy coming bring happiness!'
carries cushions to arrange the guest's seating place
